items.md: DONALD DUCK and the Mummy's Ring - 28 - Reprinted with changes by Barks from *Donald Duck* Four Color No. 29, 1943. (Mar. 10, 1965)
items.value: DONALD DUCK and the Mummy's Ring - 28 - Reprinted with changes by Barks from Donald Duck Four Color No. 29, 1943. (Mar. 10, 1965)

items.md: *Changes: Barks redrew the first, sixteenth and seventeenth pages of this story for its reprinting; however, the redrawing involved no changes in the substance of the story. Copies of the original drawings were re-inked (the shift in the inking style is noticeable) and the positions of the dialogue balloons—but not the dialogue itself—altered slightly. The only real change is in the fifth panel on the seventeenth page, where two of the nephews are drawn in new positions, rather than simply re-inked. When asked why these pages were redrawn, Barks replied, "I don't remember exactly, but I think the original printing plates were lost or damaged." (Feb. 17, 1972, letter to Michael Barrier)*
items.value: Changes: Barks redrew the first, sixteenth and seventeenth pages of this story for its reprinting; however, the redrawing involved no changes in the substance of the story. Copies of the original drawings were re-inked (the shift in the inking style is noticeable) and the positions of the dialogue balloons—but not the dialogue itself—altered slightly. The only real change is in the fifth panel on the seventeenth page, where two of the nephews are drawn in new positions, rather than simply re-inked. When asked why these pages were redrawn, Barks replied, "I don't remember exactly, but I think the original printing plates were lost or damaged." (Feb. 17, 1972, letter to Michael Barrier)
